introSection: Geopanic (Japanese: ジオパニック) was a steel enclosed roller coaster previously located at Tokyo Dome City in Bunkyo, Tokyo, Japan. The ride closed on 15 January 2008. This closure was announced as a temporary closure for refurbishment, but the coaster never reopened.

sections.title: Trains
sections.content: The train featured lap bars with over-the-shoulder restraints attached to the lap bars instead of the ride car, making for a very unusual type of restraint.

fullText:
Geopanic (Japanese: ジオパニック) was a steel enclosed roller coaster previously located at Tokyo Dome City in Bunkyo, Tokyo, Japan. The ride closed on 15 January 2008. This closure was announced as a temporary closure for refurbishment, but the coaster never reopened.



Black track and supports.

The train featured lap bars with over-the-shoulder restraints attached to the lap bars instead of the ride car, making for a very unusual type of restraint.


 * Geopanic on the parks website (Japanese)
